column8: Have you heard of "Big Ben?" It's one of the most____________landmarks in Britain. (FAME)
column9: When you think of Big Ben , what do you think of? You probably think of a clock tower on the end of a big____________in the centre of London. (BUILD)
column10: But Big Ben is_____________not a tower - it's the bell inside the tower. It's named after Sir Benjamin Hall, whose name is written on the bell. (ACTUAL)
column11: The first bell cracked and broke, so the current bell is in fact a______________. It was first rung in 1859. (REPLACE)
column12: Until 2012, the tower was officially called the Clock Tower. Then it was renamed in____________of Queen Elizabeth's 60 years as queen. (CELEBRATE)
column13: Now it is called  Elizabeth Tower. And the____________bell behind the clock has an official name too. It's not Big Ben - that's just a nickname. It's called the Great Bell. (NOISE)
column14: Toby was enjoying being a student at university. However, he had_____________no idea what job he wanted to do. (ABSOLUTE)
column15: He knew that he would have to make a_______________very soon. (DECIDE)
column16: He started thinking about all the different______________but he didn't like any of them. (POSSIBLE)
column17: He got a pen and made a long list of all the jobs he could think of - doctor, farmer, teacher,_____________, etc - and then crossed out the ones he didn't like. It was all of them. (BANK)
column18: When Toby's best friend Laura came in the room, she looked at the list, and at Toby's sad expression. Laura was very_______________and knew immediately what Toby had missed. (INTELLIGENCE)
column19: "Toby", she said. "You like coming up with lots of choices, and you know what you don't like. You would do really_______________as a policeman." (GOOD)
